judgment.summary: A high-dividend payer with strong cash flow and returns, but facing declining revenue and high leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate; next earnings date 2026-07-28.
positives: Forward P/E 12.20x — attractive relative to trailing P/E and sector peers.
Dividend yield 6.6% — substantial income for shareholders.
Free cash flow $4.63B TTM — strong cash generation supports operations and dividends.
Return on equity 33.4% — high return on shareholder capital.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $112.88.
negatives: Revenue growth -1.6% YoY and earnings growth -27.2% YoY — indicating a challenging top and bottom line.
Debt/Equity ratio 181.51% — significant leverage on the balance sheet.
Dividend payout ratio 106.2% — dividend is not fully covered by earnings.
NTSB report highlights operational concerns regarding inspection schedules.

news.paragraph: The NTSB cited a relaxed inspection schedule as a factor in an engine failure incident on a UPS plane, a development that could raise operational concerns. This news follows a recent earnings report where UPS missed on EPS and saw significant year-over-year declines in revenue and earnings growth. Traders should monitor any potential regulatory scrutiny or operational impacts stemming from the NTSB findings.

headlines.title: NTSB: Flaw that led to engine flying off UPS plane grew unnoticed under relaxed inspection schedule
